item.create_stressbound.kinetic_binder.t: Right-click a transmitter, then right-click a receiver to bind. Shift-right-click a receiver to clear its link; shift-right-click air to clear the stored transmitter.
message.create_stressbound.bind.transmit: The transmitter dimension is not available.
message.create_stressbound.bind.cross_di: Cross-dimension links are disabled by config.
message.create_stressbound.bind.transmit: The transmitter chunk is not currently loaded.
message.create_stressbound.bind.not_tran: Selected block is not a stress transmitter.
message.create_stressbound.bind.remote_l: That transmitter is attached to a remote-powered network. This link would loop.
message.create_stressbound.bind.player_l: You have reached the server link limit.
message.create_stressbound.bind.transmit: That transmitter already has the maximum number of receivers.
command.create_stressbound.links.stress_: Set link %s reserved stress to %s SU.
Set %s link(s) reserved stress to %s SU.
goggle.create_stressbound.transmitter.po: Full redstone signal is stopping output.
goggle.create_stressbound.transmitter.re: Remote loop detected; output is blocked.
goggle.create_stressbound.receiver.power: Full redstone signal is stopping input.
create_stressbound.ponder.tag.stress_lin: Components used to bridge Create rotational force between static and moving structures
create_stressbound.ponder.stressbound_li: The Stress Transmitter reads speed and spare SU from its local Create network.
Right-click the transmitter with the Kinetic Binder to store it.
Then right-click a receiver. The link reserves a configurable SU budget.
When both endpoints are loaded, redstone strength can throttle the remote speed and budget.
A full redstone signal stops an endpoint; weaker signals scale it down smoothly.
create_stressbound.ponder.stressbound_mo: Stressbound endpoints can be carried by Create Contraptions, trains, and compatible Aeronautics-style bodies.
While moving, the link follows runtime endpoint ids instead of assuming fixed block positions.
For best performance, keep both chunks loaded and avoid remote loops between receivers and transmitters.
create_stressbound.ponder.stressbound_tu: A compact motor and transmitter bank provides the remote stress source for the chassis.
The andesite cross is the moving chassis, so the four thrusters read as one body.
The center vertical receiver and gyroscopic propeller bearing form the tumbler core.
Bind that transmitter bank to all four receivers; redstone links only carry tilt signals.
The sensor outputs analog redstone on each face proportional to that side's tilt.
Each sensor face feeds a redstone-link transmitter with its own frequency pair.
Signals are crossed wirelessly: the east receiver listens to the west sensor face.
The lamps sit beside the receiver links and show each side's stop signal.
When the east side dips, the west-side sensor output is low, so the east receiver stays fast.
The high side receives the strong opposite signal and throttles down, creating a righting torque.
Place the whole template with /place template create_stressbound:stressbound/tumbler.
